q: Centrifugal pump converts mechanical energy into hydraulic energy.
The rotating impeller moves water or other fluids by using centripetal force.
When all the clothes are completely washed in a washing machine, they are spun very fast to remove excess water.
The centrifugal governor consists of two balls of unequal mass.
As the engine is working, the balls of a centrifugal governor revolve around the spindle at a certain speed.
Centripetal force is directed away from the axis of rotation or centre of curvature.
Centrifugal force in a circular motion is a real force.
Centrifugal force acts radially away from the centre of the circle.
Centrifugal force results from the effect of inertia.
The force of friction between the tires of the vehicle and the road surface allows a car to negotiate a curve.
The outward push experienced by passengers when a car takes a sudden turn is due to the centripetal force acting on passengers.
A satellite orbiting a planet is supported by the centrifugal force.
Removing the excess water by spin drying clothes in a washing machine is with the help of centrifugal force.
In Example 2.3, the radius of curvature of the arc is $6\text{ m}$.
The mass of the athlete is calculated using the formula $m = \frac{w}{g}$ where $g = 9.8\text{ m s}^{-2}$.
Frictional force between the feet and the ground provides the centrifugal force.
If the radius of curvature is smaller, the centripetal force will become smaller.
When the frictional force is less than the required centripetal force, the athlete will slip.
When a skater forms a circle, he has to lean inwards.
Centripetal force is the force acting on an object in circular motion which is directed towards the axis of rotation (or) centre of curvature.
